title: Comparing absolute values challenge
description: Practice more challenging comparison problems with absolute value.

assessment_items.question_content:
We want the following inequality to be true:  
  
 
$|x| < |-7|$  

  

 **Which of the following are possible values for $x$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The inequality $|x| < |-7|$ means that $x$ is  closer to $0$ than $-7$ is.




[[☃ image 1]]

All numbers between $-7$ and $7$ are closer to $0$ than $-7$ is.

$-2$ and $6$ are closer to $0$ than $-7$ is.



[[☃ image 1]]

We can also plug in each number for $x$ to see if the inequality is $\green{\text{true}}$:


$\begin{align}|-2| &< |-7|\\
\green{2}&\green<\green{7}\end{align}$  
$$

$\begin{align}|6| &< |-7|\\
\green{6}&\green<\green{7}\end{align}$  
$$

$\begin{align}|-8| &< |-7|\\
\red{8}&\red<\red{7}\end{align}$  
$$
$-2$ and $6$ are possible values for $x$.

assessment_items.question_content:
We want the following inequality to be true:  
  

$|x| > |-10|$  
  

 **Which of the following are possible values for $x$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The inequality $|x| > |-10|$ means that $x$ is farther away from $0$ than $-10$ is.


[[☃ image 1]]

All numbers less than $-10$ and all numbers greater than $10$ are farther away from $0$ than $-10$ is.

$-17$ and $11$ are farther from $0$ than $-10$ is.



[[☃ image 1]]

We can also plug in each number for $x$ to see if the inequality is $\green{\text{true}}$:


$\begin{align}|-17| &> |-10|\\
\green{17}&\green>\green{10}\end{align}$  
$$

$\begin{align}|-1| &> |-10|\\
\red{1}&\red>\red{10}\end{align}$   
$$  

$\,\,\,\,\begin{align}|11| &> |-10|\\
\green{11}&\green>\green{10}\end{align}$
$-17$ and $11$ are possible values for $x$.

assessment_items.question_content:
We want the following inequality to be true:  
  

$|x| > |5|$  
  

 **Which of the following are possible values for $x$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The inequality $|x| > |5|$ means that $x$ is farther away from $0$ than $5$ is.



[[☃ image 1]]

All numbers less than $-5$ and all numbers greater than $5$ are farther away from $0$ than $5$ is.

$9$ is farther from $0$ than $5$ is.



[[☃ image 1]]

We can also plug in each number for $x$ to see if the inequality is $\green{\text{true}}$:


$\begin{align}|9| &> |5|\\
\green{9}&\green>\green{5}\end{align}$  
$$

$\begin{align}|3| &> |5|\\
\red{3}&\red>\red{5}\end{align}$   
$$  

$\begin{align}|-4| &> |5|\\
\red{4}&\red>\red{5}\end{align}$   
$$
$9$ is a possible value for $x$.

assessment_items.question_content:
We want the following inequality to be true:  
  
 
 $|x| < |14|$  
  

 **Which of the following are possible values for $x$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The inequality $|x| < |14|$ means that $x$ is  closer to $0$ than $14$ is.



[[☃ image 1]]

All numbers between $-14$ and $14$ are closer to $0$ than $14$ is.

$-12$, $0$, and $5$ are closer to $0$ than $14$ is.



[[☃ image 1]]

We can also plug in each number for $x$ to see if the inequality is $\green{\text{true}}$:


$\begin{align}|-12| &< |14|\\
\green{12}&\green<\green{14}\end{align}$  
$$

$\begin{align}|0| &< |14|\\
\green{0}&\green<\green{14}\end{align}$  
$$

$\begin{align}|5| &< |14|\\
\green{5}&\green<\green{14}\end{align}$  
$$
$-12$, $0$, and $5$ are possible values for $x$.

assessment_items.question_content:
We want the following inequality to be true:  
  
 
$|x |< 9$  
  

 **Which of the following are possible values for $x$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The inequality $|x| < 9$ means that $x$ is closer to $0$ than $9$ is.



[[☃ image 1]]

All numbers between $-9$ and $9$ are closer to $0$ than $9$ is.

$7$'s distance from $0$ is less than $9$.



[[☃ image 1]]

We can also plug in each number for $x$ to see if the inequality is $\green{\text{true}}$:


$\begin{align}|7| &< 9\\
\green{7}&\green<\green{9}\end{align}$  
$$

$\begin{align}|-10| &< 9\\
\red{10}&\red<\red{9}\end{align}$  
$$

$\begin{align}|13| &< 9\\
\red{13}&\red<\red{9}\end{align}$  
$$
$7$ is a possible value for $x$.

assessment_items.question_content:
We want the following inequality to be true:  
  
 
$|x |> -16$  
  

 **Which of the following are possible values for $x$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Absolute value is always positive.  So, $|x|$ is a positive number.  

$-16$ is a negative number.

A positive number is always greater than a negative number.

So, we can plug any number for $x$ and make the inequality true.

We can also plug in the given numbers for $x$ to see if the inequality is $\green{\text{true}}$:


$\begin{align}|19| &>-16\\
\green{19}&\green>\green{-16}\end{align}$  
$$

$\begin{align}|3| &>-16\\
\green{3}&\green>\green{-16}\end{align}$  
$$

$\begin{align}|-15| &>-16\\
\green{15}&\green>\green{-16}\end{align}$  
$$
$-15$, $3$, and $19$ are possible values for $x$.

assessment_items.question_content:
We want the following inequality to be true:  
  
 
$x < |-3|$  
  

 **Which of the following are possible values for $x$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$|-3|=3$

So, $x< |-3|$ is the same as $x<3$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$-8$ and $0$ are less than $3$.



[[☃ image 1]]

We can also plug in each number for $x$ to see if the inequality is $\green{\text{true}}$:


$\begin{align}-8&< |-3|\\
\green{-8}&\green<\green{3}\end{align}$  
$$

$\begin{align}0&< |-3|\\
\green{0}&\green<\green{3}\end{align}$  
$$

$\begin{align}4&< |-3|\\
\red{4}&\red<\red{3}\end{align}$  
$$
$-8$ and $0$ are possible values for $x$.

assessment_items.question_content:
We want the following inequality to be true:  
   

$x >|2|$  
  

 **Which of the following are possible values for $x$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$|2|=2$

So, $x> |2|$ is the same as $x>2$.



[[☃ image 1]]

$5$ is greater than $2$.



[[☃ image 1]]

We can also plug in each number for $x$ to see if the inequality is $\green{\text{true}}$:


$\begin{align}5&> |2|\\
\green{5}&\green>\green{2}\end{align}$  
$$

$\begin{align}1&> |2|\\
\red{1}&\red>\red{2}\end{align}$  
$$

$\begin{align}-9&> |2|\\
\red{-9}&\red>\red{2}\end{align}$  
$$
$5$ is a possible value for $x$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the following number line to determine if the expressions are true or false.**  


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ categorizer 1]]
hints:
First, let's plot $|\blue{a}|$ and $|\pink{b}|$.  Absolute value is a number's distance from $0$.  



[[☃ image 1]]

Remember, distance is always zero or positive.
On a number line, numbers to the right are greater than numbers to the left.
[[☃ categorizer 1]]

options.alt: A number line with evenly spaced tick marks. The tick mark in the middle is labeled 0. A closed point labeled a is plotted 3 tick marks to the left of 0. A closed point labeled b is plotted 1 tick mark to the right of 0.
